step_by_step_thinking:
Lesions of oral tuberculosis typically include central caseation, giant cells, and the presence of epithelioid cells. Hyaline degeneration is less characteristic of oral tuberculosis compared to other types of infections like candidiasis or viral infections. Therefore, hyaline degeneration would be the exception among the given options.
\boxed{B} Based on the description of oral tuberculosis lesions and comparing them against common changes seen in other oral conditions, hyaline degeneration stands out as being less likely to occur specifically in cases of oral tuberculosis.
